title: cerasus avium f. decumana
intro: Cerasus avium f. decumana, also known as the wild cherry or bird cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Cerasus avium f. decumana include reducing fever, treating coughs and colds, and providing relief from digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its bark can also be used to create a decoction or salve for external use.
